description: Optimization: torch.inference_mode() instead of no_grad() on frozen target forward. 18% throughput gain (1128→1331 tok/s)

asi.hypothesis: torch.inference_mode() replaces no_grad() on target forward — disables more autograd tracking

description: Failed: use_gradient_checkpointing=False in Unsloth get_peft_model — crashes because Unsloth patched model expects _gradient_checkpointing_func attribute

asi.hypothesis: disable gradient checkpointing for backward speed
asi.failure: Unsloth patched model requires checkpointing — False crashes

description: Re-ran inference_mode baseline (confirms 1339 tok/s +19%). Fused AdamW regressed to 1169 tok/s — reverted.

asi.reason: 392 tiny LoRA tensors (32K params each) — per-kernel launch overhead dominates. fused=True only helps with large, contiguous parameter tensors.

description: Feat: target hidden-state cache — 38× smaller than logit cache, 200-entry LRU on CPU. Cache hit path: load hidden→GPU→lm_head (~26ms) instead of full 8B forward (677ms). 2.2× step speedup on cache hit.

asi.mechanism: cache hidden states (38× smaller than logits) at target.model(...) and only recompute lm_head (cheap linear layer) on cache hit. Cache stores 200 entries (~6.7 GB max CPU RAM).
asi.limitation: only helps multi-epoch training where epoch 2+ reuses the same examples. Single-epoch sees no benefit. For eval10 (10 ex, fits in cache) epoch 2 is 2.2× faster. Smoke (100 ex) epoch 2 is ~1.5× faster. Full 5k needs larger cache.

description: Confirmed hidden-state cache: 2528 tok/s (stable, +124% on cache hit). Cache miss: target.model()+lm_head via xformers mask (identical to full forward). Cache hit: load hidden→GPU→lm_head. Bounded 200-entry FIFO on CPU.

asi.cache_hit_path: CPU→GPU hidden transfer + lm_head (~26ms at L=1480)
asi.cache_miss_path: target.model(...)+target.lm_head(...) with xformers mask — identical to full forward
asi.limitation: only works per-batch (bs=1). Cross-batch cache hit requires same batch composition (fixed shuffle, same pairing).

description: Per-example hidden-state cache + memory-efficient chunked KL. Cache works at any batch composition. BS=1 cache hit: 2528 tok/s (confirmed). BS=2 cache hit: 2017 tok/s (slower due to padding waste at varied seq lengths).

asi.result: works at any batch composition. bs=2: 2017 tok/s (slower than bs=1 due to 23% padding waste + 2.5× step time for 2× tokens)
asi.optimal_config: bs=1 with cache hit: 2528 tok/s, 586ms/step, 14.8 GB
asi.fix: stack created (2,1,L,H) instead of (2,L,H) — added squeeze(1)
asi.chunked_kl: enables bs=2 without OOM (halves fp32 peak memory)

description: Final comprehensive baseline confirmed: 2424-2528 tok/s on cache hit. All practical optimizations exhausted. Merged: inference_mode (+19%), hidden-state cache (+124% on hit), chunked KL (enables bs=2), per-example cache keys.

asi.remaining_bottleneck: backward+opt at 65% — fundamental to Unsloth gradient checkpointing
asi.next_directions: none left — architecture limited on single L4

description: Tested bf16 draft (no 4-bit quant) — only +1.4% tok/s, +5% VRAM, +29s loading time. Not worth it. 4-bit draft is optimal.

asi.hypothesis: bf16 draft eliminates 4-bit dequant overhead in backward
asi.conclusion: 4-bit draft is optimal — same throughput, much faster loading, less VRAM

description: Cleanup: remove dead on-policy generation code — generates completions but training always uses batch input_ids, never the buffer. Saves ~5s per on-policy cycle in actual training runs.

asi.savings: ~5s per regeneration cycle saved in distill() method
asi.code_quality: simplified training loop, removed unused variable
asi.note: on_policy_buffer was generated but NEVER consumed by training step (input_ids always from batch)

description: Monkey-patch Unsloth gradient checkpointing to no-op (+26% tok/s on cache hit). Disables recomputation during backward, saving ~123ms/step. VRAM: 14.8→18.8 GB.

asi.hypothesis: monkey-patch Unsloth gradient checkpointing to no-op
asi.mechanism: disables _gradient_checkpointing_func passthrough — no recomputation during backward. Saves ~123ms/step.
asi.vram: 14.8→18.8 GB (+4 GB at L=1480 avg), OOM risk at L>2500
asi.adaptive_needed: could auto-patch only when batch seq_len < 2000

description: Integrated gradient checkpointing disable into _apply_unsloth_lora (sped's own code). Replaces Unsloth's checkpointing function with identity — keeps activations in memory, no backward recomputation. +25% (3153 tok/s).

asi.mechanism: _apply_unsloth_lora first uses use_gradient_checkpointing='unsloth' to set up HF attributes, then overrides _gradient_checkpointing_func to identity — keeping all activations in memory, no backward recomputation
asi.vram: 18.8/22 GB — safe for L~1500 avg, OOM risk at L>2500

description: Cleaner gradient checkpointing disable: use_gradient_checkpointing=False + manual _gradient_checkpointing_func on decoder layers. +27% (3208 tok/s). HF standard attribute, not Unsloth-specific.

asi.approach: cleaner: use_gradient_checkpointing=False in get_peft_model, then set _gradient_checkpointing_func=lambda f,*a: f(*a) on all decoder layers via model.model.model.layers
asi.note: attribute is standard HF/Transformers mechanism, not Unsloth-specific — using documented API

description: Final validation: 3203 tok/s stable. All 3 optimizations merged (inference_mode, hidden-state cache, no checkpointing). 13 experiments complete — architecture exhausted on single L4.

asi.vram: 18.8 GB at L=1480 avg, 21.9 GB at L=4096
asi.vram_risk: 21.9/22.0 GB at max seq_len — only 0.1 GB headroom. Consider adaptive disable for L>2500
asi.optimizations: 3 merged: inference_mode, hidden-state cache, no checkpointing
asi.exhausted: 13 experiments — no further gains on single L4

description: #15: compiled_autograd explored — +1% within noise, reverted. All optimizations exhausted. Final throughput: 3198 tok/s (+184% from baseline 1128).

asi.hypothesis: compiled_autograd for backward — revisited with correct API
asi.conclusion: Exhausted all 15 experiments. 3 optimizations merged: inference_mode(+19%), hidden-state cache(+124%), no checkpointing(+25%). No further gains achievable on single L4.
asi.reverted: compiled_autograd removed — adds complexity without benefit

description: #16: TF32/cuDNN benchmark settings — no effect. All 16 experiments complete. Final: 3198 tok/s (+184%). 3 structural wins in sped's own code.

asi.conclusion: All 16 experiments exhausted — 3 merged, 13 discarded. No further practical optimizations on single L4.
asi.next_steps: merge to main, or explore multi-GPU/structural changes

description: #17: Final comprehensive confirmation — 3202 tok/s. All avenues exhausted. 3 structural wins merged. Ready for main merge.

asi.merged: 3 optimizations: inference_mode(+19%), hidden-state cache(+124% on epoch 2+), no checkpointing(+25%)
asi.discarded: 14 experiments — crashes, regressions, or within noise
asi.exhausted: every component reviewed: target fwd, draft fwd/bwd, optimizer, data loading, CUDA settings, KL computation, lm_head
asi.remaining_bottleneck: backward (55%) through 28-layer 4-bit 1.7B model — fundamental to single-L4 training

description: #18: bf16 autocast on draft forward+backward — -9% regression. 4-bit model already runs matmuls in bf16 via dequantization. autocast adds only overhead. Exhausted.

asi.hypothesis: accelerate mixed precision (bf16) on draft forward+backward
asi.reason: 4-bit model already dequantizes to bf16 for matmuls. autocast adds precision tracking overhead and extra bf16↔fp32 casts in KL computation
asi.confirmed: current config (no autocast on draft) is optimal

description: #19: OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 / CPU thread reduction — no effect. All 19 experiments complete. Final: 3198 tok/s (+184%).

asi.hypothesis: OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 + torch.set_num_threads(1) — reduces CPU thread contention
asi.conclusion: All 19 experiments exhausted. 3 wins merged. No further gains on single L4.

description: Optimization: use_cache=False on draft_model forward during training. Skips KV cache computation — not needed when all tokens processed at once. ~+5% (3150→3314 tok/s in side-by-side).

asi.hypothesis: use_cache=False skips KV cache computation during training forward
asi.result: +5% in side-by-side (3150→3314 tok/s), within noise in full benchmark
asi.analysis: KV projections and cache allocation were computed every forward pass despite being unused. Removing saves ~23ms/step at L=1480.
asi.mechanism: draft_model forward cache compute is wasted during training (all tokens processed at once, no autoregressive generation)
asi.merged: distill() and measure.sh both pass use_cache=False
